text:
INPATIENT HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY PROGRESS NOTE
Day 21 of Induction Chemotherapy — Count Nadir Assessment

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Ms. Margaret Okonkwo is a 48-year-old woman with newly diagnosed AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse risk, currently admitted to the inpatient hematology unit for ongoing supportive care following 7+3 induction chemotherapy plus midostaurin. She is at day 21 of induction. This note represents the formal nadir assessment.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Okonkwo presented approximately 3 weeks ago with a 2-week history of fatigue, progressive dyspnea on exertion, gingival bleeding, easy bruising, and low-grade fevers. Workup at that time revealed a white blood cell count of 84,000/uL, hemoglobin 7.1 g/dL, and platelets of 28,000/uL. Peripheral blood smear demonstrated circulating blasts with Auer rods. She was admitted urgently and underwent bone marrow biopsy and aspirate, which confirmed AML with 62% myeloblasts by flow cytometry. Cytogenetics showed 46,XX with a minor del(7q) clone in 2 of 20 metaphases. Next-generation sequencing demonstrated FLT3-ITD with a very high allelic ratio (VAR 0.82), along with co-mutations in DNMT3A p.R882H, ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12, SRSF2 p.P95H, and RUNX1 p.R204Q — a mutational profile consistent with AML-MRC arising from antecedent MDS. She carries a history of MDS with refractory cytopenia with multilineage dysplasia (RCMD) diagnosed approximately 18 months prior, which had been observed without treatment. Given the FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio and the overall adverse ELN 2022 risk profile (FLT3-ITD HAR + DNMT3A + ASXL1 + SRSF2 + RUNX1 co-mutations + AML-MRC features), she was initiated on 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day continuous infusion days 1-7, daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV days 1-3) with midostaurin 50 mg PO BID added on days 8-21 per the RATIFY protocol for FLT3-mutated AML. Allogeneic stem cell transplant in first remission remains the long-term goal.

Her induction course has been complicated by febrile neutropenia on approximately day 10, with blood cultures growing E. coli (1 of 2 bottles), treated with a completed 14-day course of cefepime (completed as of several days ago). She also developed Grade 3 mucositis requiring morphine PCA for pain control and total parenteral nutrition for approximately 8 days. She had Grade 2 nausea and vomiting managed with ondansetron and lorazepam. She required frequent transfusion support — packed red blood cells to maintain hemoglobin above 8 g/dL, and platelets 2-3 times per week during the nadir period. A transient Grade 1 transaminase elevation (ALT peak 78 U/L) was noted and has since trended down. No midostaurin dose reductions were required. No cardiac toxicity was observed.

At today's assessment (day 21), she is clinically improving. Fevers have been absent for over 10 days. Mucositis is improving significantly — she is now tolerating oral liquids without significant discomfort and has discontinued PCA morphine, transitioning to oral acetaminophen. She is off TPN, tolerating a soft diet. She reports ongoing fatigue, which is expected at this stage. She has no headache, no chest pain, no shortness of breath at rest. She denies any new rash or skin changes. Bowel movements have resumed. ANC remains at 0/uL as of this morning's complete blood count, consistent with expected aplasia at day 21. No circulating blasts were identified on the peripheral blood differential. Midostaurin was continued through day 21 (today) and is now complete per protocol.

Bone marrow biopsy will be deferred to day 28-30 per institutional protocol, as marrow at day 21 is expected to show profound hypocellularity and is not interpretable for response assessment in the setting of ongoing aplasia. We will reassess timing based on CBC recovery.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

(+) Fatigue — significant, improving compared to week 2.
(+) Mild dysphagia and mouth soreness — substantially improved from peak mucositis.
(+) Mild anorexia — improving; tolerating soft diet.
(-) Fever — none for >10 days.
(-) Chills or rigors — none.
(-) Rash, new skin changes — none.
(-) Chest pain — none.
(-) Shortness of breath at rest — none (mild exertional dyspnea persists, likely anemia-related).
(-) Headache or focal neurologic symptoms — none.
(-) Visual changes — none.
(-) Nausea and vomiting — minimal, managed.
(-) Diarrhea — none; bowel movements normal.
(-) Hematochezia or melena — none.
(-) Gingival or mucosal bleeding — none (compared to presentation).
(-) Hematuria — none.
(-) Joint pain or swelling — none.
(-) Cough — none.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY

1. Myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) with refractory cytopenia with multilineage dysplasia (RCMD) — diagnosed approximately 18 months prior; managed with observation (no prior HMA therapy or lenalidomide). Transformed to AML-MRC at current admission.
2. Hypertension — well-controlled on lisinopril prior to admission; lisinopril held during induction given hypotension risk and renal monitoring requirements.
3. Type 2 diabetes mellitus — managed on metformin prior to admission; metformin held during hospitalization; glucose managed with insulin sliding scale as needed (hyperglycemia modest, no insulin infusion required).
4. No prior solid organ malignancy.
5. No prior radiation therapy.
6. No prior cardiac disease; echocardiogram performed pre-induction demonstrated LVEF 62%, no wall motion abnormalities, cleared for anthracycline.

---

SOCIAL HISTORY

Ms. Okonkwo is a 48-year-old woman of Nigerian descent, born in Lagos and residing in the United States for approximately 20 years. She works as a licensed practical nurse. She is married with two adult children. She is a lifetime never-smoker. She drinks alcohol rarely — typically a glass of wine on social occasions, no regular use. She denies recreational drug use. She has a strong support system; her husband has been present throughout the hospitalization and is actively involved in care discussions. She has expressed understanding of and interest in pursuing allogeneic stem cell transplant in first remission, given the high-risk nature of her disease. Insurance coverage confirmed. Social work has been involved to assist with coordination of leave from work and home care planning for post-discharge period.

---

FAMILY HISTORY

Father: deceased at age 71 from prostate cancer.
Mother: alive, age 74, with type 2 diabetes mellitus and hypertension.
Siblings: one brother (age 52, alive, healthy) — HLA typing has been requested as part of donor workup; results pending. One sister (age 44, alive, history of sickle cell trait, otherwise healthy).
No known family history of AML, MDS, or other hematologic malignancies.
No known germline predisposition syndromes identified in family. Genetic counseling referral has been placed given age of AML onset and co-mutation profile; DNMT3A R882H is primarily somatic in this context, but evaluation is appropriate.

---

ALLERGIES

No known drug allergies (NKDA).

---

MEDICATIONS (Inpatient, Current)

1. Midostaurin (Rydapt) 50 mg PO BID — FLT3 inhibitor; per protocol, day 8-21 of induction (last dose today, day 21). To be continued during consolidation and maintenance phases as applicable.
2. Fluconazole 400 mg PO/IV daily — antifungal prophylaxis.
3. Acyclovir 400 mg PO BID — HSV/VZV prophylaxis.
4. Levofloxacin 500 mg PO daily — antibacterial prophylaxis (neutropenic precautions).
5. Ondansetron 8 mg IV/PO q8h PRN — antiemetic.
6. Lorazepam 0.5 mg PO/IV q6h PRN — antiemetic/anxiolytic.
7. Acetaminophen 650 mg PO q6h PRN — pain management (mucositis, transitioned from morphine PCA).
8. Insulin lispro sliding scale — glycemic management (metformin held).
9. Omeprazole 40 mg PO daily — GI prophylaxis.
10. Normal saline IV maintenance — hydration support (reduced from aggressive induction hydration).
11. Cefepime 2 g IV q8h — completed 14-day course for E. coli bacteremia; DISCONTINUED as of day 17.
12. Packed red blood cells — transfusion support PRN (Hgb trigger < 8 g/dL).
13. Apheresis platelets — transfusion support PRN (platelet trigger < 10,000/uL, or < 20,000/uL with active mucositis/fever).

Prior home medications held: Lisinopril 10 mg daily (held — resumption planned post-discharge); Metformin 1000 mg BID (held — resumption planned post-discharge when oral intake fully normalized and renal function confirmed stable).

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

General: Alert, oriented, cooperative. Fatigued-appearing but in no acute distress. Speaks in full sentences. Color somewhat pale but improved compared to admission.

Vital Signs: Temperature 36.8°C (afebrile); Blood pressure 118/72 mmHg; Heart rate 88 beats per minute; Respiratory rate 16 breaths per minute; SpO2 97% on room air; Weight 68 kg (down approximately 2 kg from admission, expected given induction-related catabolism and mucositis).

HEENT: Anicteric sclerae. Oropharynx: mild residual erythema of buccal mucosa; no active ulcers; no pseudomembrane formation; saliva present. No gingival bleeding observed (a significant improvement from presentation). No cervical lymphadenopathy.

Chest/Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezing, rales, or rhonchi. Chest wall non-tender.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No jugular venous distension. Peripheral pulses 2+ bilaterally.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Bowel sounds present in all quadrants. No hepatomegaly on palpation. Mild splenomegaly on palpation at the left costal margin (consistent with known mild splenomegaly at diagnosis, likely unchanged or improving). No ascites.

Extremities: No peripheral edema. No petechiae or purpura on lower extremities (improved from admission). No ecchymoses on upper extremities beyond healing bruises. No erythema or induration along any vascular access sites. PICC line in left upper arm — site clean and dry, no signs of infection.

Skin: Pale but intact. No new rash. Mild residual petechiae on lower legs (fading). No jaundice.

Neurologic: Alert and oriented x4. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. No focal deficits. Gait not formally assessed today given fatigue, but patient ambulatory to restroom with minimal assistance.

Musculoskeletal: No bony tenderness on palpation of sternum, ribs, or long bones (a useful proxy for bone marrow pressure; negative, consistent with aplastic marrow rather than disease expansion).

---

LABORATORY RESULTS

Complete Blood Count (today, day 21):
- WBC: 0.2 x 10^3/uL (ANC: 0/uL — profound neutropenia)
- Hemoglobin: 8.3 g/dL (post-transfusion; pre-transfusion nadir this week was 7.4 g/dL)
- Hematocrit: 25.1%
- Platelets: 9,000/uL (transfusion given this morning; pre-transfusion nadir: 4,000/uL)
- Differential: No blasts identified on manual differential; 100% lymphocytes (residual lymphocytes, consistent with post-aplastic pattern)

Reticulocyte count: 0.1% (markedly suppressed — expected in aplasia).

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium 138 mEq/L, Potassium 3.7 mEq/L, Chloride 102 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 24 mEq/L
- BUN 14 mg/dL, Creatinine 0.78 mg/dL (eGFR > 60; renal function preserved)
- Glucose 128 mg/dL (mild hyperglycemia; insulin sliding scale managed)
- Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL (normal), Direct bilirubin 0.2 mg/dL
- AST 34 U/L, ALT 48 U/L (resolving from peak ALT 78 U/L on day 14; trending down)
- Alkaline phosphatase 82 U/L
- Albumin 3.1 g/dL (mildly reduced — expected in context of prolonged hospitalization and catabolic state; improving from nadir of 2.7 g/dL during peak mucositis/TPN period)
- Total protein 5.9 g/dL

Coagulation:
- PT 12.4 s, INR 1.1, PTT 28 s (normal)
- Fibrinogen 298 mg/dL (no DIC)

LDH: 184 U/L (markedly improved from 890 U/L at diagnosis — a favorable trend; no blast-level LDH elevation).

Uric acid: 3.2 mg/dL (normal; allopurinol discontinued early per protocol, no residual hyperuricemia).

Tacrolimus trough: N/A (not yet on tacrolimus; this is pre-transplant phase).

Fluconazole level: not routinely checked.

Blood cultures (from last 7 days): No growth to date. Most recent set drawn on day 17 (at time of last fever spike, low-grade); negative at 5 days.

Urinalysis: No evidence of infection. No hematuria.

CMV PCR: Negative (baseline; relevant given anticipated transplant trajectory).

---

IMAGING RESULTS

No new imaging obtained today. For reference:
- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis performed at staging prior to induction: mild splenomegaly (14.2 cm), no extramedullary disease, no hepatomegaly, no lymphadenopathy — previously reviewed and noted.
- 2D echocardiogram performed pre-induction: LVEF 62%, normal cardiac function; anthracycline cleared — previously reviewed and noted.
- No indication for repeat imaging at this juncture.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Margaret Okonkwo is a 48-year-old woman with newly diagnosed AML with myelodysplasia-related changes, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (VAF 0.82), ELN 2022 adverse-risk disease (co-mutations DNMT3A R882H, ASXL1 frameshift, SRSF2 P95H, RUNX1 R204Q), arising from antecedent RCMD-type MDS. She completed 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine + daunorubicin) with midostaurin days 8-21 per RATIFY protocol. Today is day 21.

1. RESPONSE ASSESSMENT / BONE MARROW BIOPSY PLANNING:
The patient is at the expected nadir at day 21. No circulating blasts on peripheral smear today — an encouraging sign, though peripheral blood clearance at this stage is not sufficient to confirm or exclude marrow response. Bone marrow biopsy at day 21 would be premature, as the marrow is anticipated to be profoundly hypocellular and cannot be reliably evaluated for blast percentage in the setting of ongoing aplasia. We will target the bone marrow biopsy for day 28-30, contingent on the trajectory of count recovery. CBC will be monitored daily; if WBC begins to rise by day 25-27, we will proceed to biopsy on day 28. If no count recovery signal is apparent by day 28, the biopsy will still proceed to assess for hypoplasia versus residual disease. The absence of circulating blasts and the degree of LDH decline (from 890 U/L at diagnosis to 184 U/L today) are both consistent with anticipated disease clearance, though firm response assessment awaits marrow biopsy with flow cytometry MRD testing.

2. CYTOPENIAS / TRANSFUSION SUPPORT:
- Hemoglobin 8.3 g/dL (post-transfusion). Transfuse pRBCs to maintain Hgb > 8 g/dL. Patient symptomatic with exertional dyspnea; target Hgb > 8.5 g/dL for comfort.
- Platelets 9,000/uL. Maintain platelet threshold of > 10,000/uL in the absence of fever or active bleeding; with current residual mucositis, maintain > 20,000/uL. Transfused this morning; reassess tomorrow.
- Continue daily CBC.
- G-CSF is not routinely administered following induction chemotherapy for AML (risk of stimulating any residual disease); hold unless specifically indicated.

3. INFECTIOUS DISEASE / FEBRILE NEUTROPENIA:
- Patient afebrile for >10 days. E. coli bacteremia treated with a full 14-day course of cefepime, completed. No active infection.
- Continue levofloxacin prophylaxis, fluconazole, and acyclovir while neutropenic.
- If fever recurs (temperature > 38.3°C) or patient develops clinical signs of infection, restart empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics (cefepime 2g IV q8h) and draw blood cultures x2.
- Low threshold for antifungal escalation to voriconazole or micafungin if fever fails to respond to antibacterial therapy after 4-5 days, or if CT imaging suggests invasive fungal infection.
- Patient on neutropenic precautions; no fresh fruits/vegetables, low-microbial diet; meticulous hand hygiene reinforced with all staff and visitors.

4. MUCOSITIS:
- Grade 3 mucositis at peak; now substantially improved (Grade 1-2 at this assessment). Patient tolerating soft diet. TPN discontinued. PCA discontinued; managing with oral acetaminophen.
- Maintain good oral hygiene with sodium bicarbonate and saline rinses.
- Continue topical care as tolerated.
- Expected to fully resolve with count recovery.

5. MIDOSTAURIN:
- Midostaurin 50 mg BID (days 8-21) completed today, day 21, per RATIFY protocol.
- Midostaurin will be re-initiated as part of consolidation (days 8-21 of HiDAC cycle) and then as post-consolidation maintenance, pending confirmation of CR and transplant planning discussions.

6. GLYCEMIC MANAGEMENT:
- Mild hyperglycemia (glucose 128 mg/dL today). Managing with insulin sliding scale.
- Metformin remains held. Will reassess for resumption post-discharge once oral intake is fully re-established and renal function confirmed stable.

7. CARDIOVASCULAR:
- Blood pressure 118/72 mmHg — well-controlled without lisinopril. Lisinopril to be restarted post-discharge.
- No signs of cardiac toxicity from daunorubicin at this time.

8. TRANSPLANT PLANNING (INITIATED):
- Given the adverse ELN risk profile (FLT3-ITD HAR, DNMT3A/ASXL1/SRSF2/RUNX1 co-mutations, AML-MRC), allogeneic stem cell transplant in first complete remission is strongly indicated and represents the only curative strategy beyond induction alone in this setting.
- HLA typing of the patient has been performed. Sibling typing initiated (brother and sister). Preliminary results expected within the week.
- Unrelated donor search via National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP/Be The Match) to be activated if no matched sibling is identified.
- BMT team formally consulted; planning discussion deferred until CR confirmed by bone marrow biopsy.
- Goal: proceed to alloSCT in CR1 as soon as a suitable donor is identified and the patient is medically fit.

9. SUPPORTIVE CARE / QUALITY OF LIFE:
- Patient is psychologically resilient and has excellent family support. She understands the gravity of her diagnosis and the rationale for transplant.
- Social work engaged. Leave from work formally requested by patient's husband.
- Chaplaincy services offered; patient declined at this time but will revisit.
- Nutritional status improving; dietitian to reassess caloric goals given resumption of oral intake.

10. DISPOSITION:
- Patient to remain admitted pending count recovery and bone marrow biopsy. Anticipated discharge approximately day 30-35 if CR confirmed and counts recovering satisfactorily.
- Discharge planning to include education on neutropenic precautions at home, wound care for PICC, and early return precautions.

Overall clinical trajectory: anticipated aplasia with favorable early signals (no circulating blasts, LDH normalization, afebrile, recovering). No features concerning for primary refractory disease at this juncture. Will reassess definitively with bone marrow biopsy at day 28-30.

---
Attending Hematologist/Oncologist
Inpatient Hematology Service
